title: Logical error: Cannot clone block with columns because block...
description:
### Describe the bug
Seems not related to https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/80415, the minimized example there doesn't reproduce anymore.
### How to reproduce
Start MinIO and run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Int) ENGINE = S3(s3, format = 'CSV', filename = 'file{_partition_id}') PARTITION BY (c0);
ALTER TABLE t0 ADD COLUMN c1 Variant(Int,String);
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c0) VALUES (1); ---Logical error
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```
<Fatal> : Logical error: 'Cannot clone block with columns because block [c0 Int32 Int32(size = 0)] has 1 columns, but 2 columns given [Int32(size = 1), Variant(size = 1, UInt8(size = 1), UInt64(size = 1), Int32(size = 0), String(size = 0))]'.
<Fatal> : Stack trace (when copying this message, always include the lines below):

2. src/Common/Exception.h:123: DB::Exception::Exception(String&&, int, String, bool) @ 0x000000000d22d18e
```

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (c0 INTEGER, c1 TEXT);
ALTER TABLE t1 ADD COLUMN c2 FLOAT;
INSERT INTO t1 (c0, c1, c2) VALUES (2, 'example', NULL);
SELECT c0, c1, c2 FROM t1 WHERE c2 IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Cloning block with mismatched column counts causes logical error
